generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance MotorShaftEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Motor-mounted encoder providing direct shaft measurement for position feedback"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorShaftEncoderData
]

instance PostGearboxEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Secondary encoder positioned post-gearbox to mitigate backlash and reduce positional uncertainty"
    DTDFVocab:producedData PostGearboxEncoderData
]

instance MotorCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor measuring input current to the permanent magnet synchronous motor"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorCurrentData
]

instance OutputAngleSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor measuring output angle from the gearbox mechanism"
    DTDFVocab:producedData OutputAngleData
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance MotorShaftEncoderData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorShaftEncoder
]

instance PostGearboxEncoderData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PostGearboxEncoder
]

instance MotorCurrentData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorCurrentSensor
]

instance OutputAngleData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OutputAngleSensor
]

instance RealTimeSensorDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorShaftEncoder, PostGearboxEncoder, MotorCurrentSensor, OutputAngleSensor
    base:desc "Aggregated real-time time-series data stream transmitted via MQTT with optimized sampling frequencies for low-latency feedback"
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance FrictionParameterModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Multi-physics-aware model for continuous derivation of joint friction parameters using aggregated data traces and physical models"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingEnabler, SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance RobotMotionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Real-time robot motion model adjusted based on friction analysis for accuracy improvement"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingEnabler, SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance CausalRelationshipModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Model detecting causal relationships between system components (e.g., friction sources) to inform corrective actions"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingEnabler
]

instance SensorDataAggregation : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated contextual data traces from physical system sensors including position, current, and angle measurements"
    DTDFVocab:fromData RealTimeSensorDataStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler
]

instance FrictionAnalysisData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Derived friction parameters and analysis results from continuous model-based calculations"
    DTDFVocab:fromData RealTimeSensorDataStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingEnabler, SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance MotionAdjustmentData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time motion adjustment parameters based on friction analysis"
    DTDFVocab:fromData FrictionAnalysisData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ControlLogicEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLModelingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML-based modeling environment supporting multi-level abstraction (requirements, functional, logical, product layers) using RFLP/OOSEM methodologies"
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionParameterService, CausalAnalysisService, MotionOptimizationService
]

instance SimulinkSimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink environment for behavioral modeling and simulation of robot motion with friction compensation"
    DTDFVocab:enables MotionOptimizationService, FrictionAnalysisService
]

instance DataProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Data aggregation and preprocessing pipeline handling MQTT streams with filtering/normalization for model compatibility"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataCollectionService
]

instance ControlLogicEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Closed-loop control logic implementation using C++/Java for real-time motion adjustments"
    DTDFVocab:enables MotionOptimizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataCollectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automated one-way data collection from physical system via passive data acquisition paradigm"
    DTDFVocab:provides PhysicalStateInsight
]

instance FrictionParameterService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous derivation of joint friction parameters using model-based calculations"
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionParameterInsight
]

instance CausalAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Detection of causal relationships between system components to inform corrective actions"
    DTDFVocab:provides ComponentRelationshipInsight
]

instance MotionOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time adjustments to robot motion based on friction analysis"
    DTDFVocab:provides MotionOptimizationAction
]

instance FrictionAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Model-based friction analysis leveraging Simulink for accuracy improvement"
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionAnalysisInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance PhysicalStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Aggregated contextual data traces representing current physical system state"
]

instance FrictionParameterInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuously derived joint friction parameters with model conformance validation"
]

instance ComponentRelationshipInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detected causal relationships between system components (e.g., friction sources) with potential corrective actions"
]

instance MotionOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Real-time adjustments to robot motion parameters based on friction analysis"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance FrictionAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detailed friction analysis results with visualization-ready data for user interaction"
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor providing torque generation with individual axis control capabilities"
]

instance GearboxMechanism : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Gearbox mechanism for torque transmission and gear reduction with backlash mitigation"
]

// C1: Environment
instance RobotMechanicalSystemEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Mechanical system environment containing all physical and digital components for the serial robot MABI MAX"
    base:contains PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor, GearboxMechanism,
                 MotorShaftEncoder, PostGearboxEncoder,
                 MotorCurrentSensor, OutputAngleSensor
]

// C10: Additional Models (from twinning process)
instance RequirementsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML requirements model defining system specifications and constraints"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingEnabler
]

instance FunctionalArchitectureModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML functional architecture model using ACME/AADL for component-connector perspective"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingEnabler
]

instance LogicalArchitectureModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML logical architecture model defining interfaces and interconnections"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingEnabler
]

instance StateMachineModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML v1.7 state machine model for behavioral modeling with MontiArc Automaton integration"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingEnabler, SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system’s orchestration and coordination rely on **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, integrating heterogeneous models (e.g., **SysML** for architecture, **MontiArc Automaton** or **state machines in SysML v1.7** for behavior, and simulation tools like **MathWorks Simulink** or general-purpose languages such as **C++/Java** for detailed behavior). The system leverages **architecture description languages (ADLs)** like **ACME**, **AADL**, or **MontiArc** to define component interfaces, interconnections, and composition, enabling modular integration. Resource management and system-wide coordination are implied through these ADLs and MBSE frameworks, which support hierarchical decomposition (e.g., **RFLP**, **OOSEM**, or **SMArDT** methodologies) to handle complexity. The document does not specify orchestration protocols (e.g., event-driven, service-oriented) but emphasizes the need for integrating diverse stakeholders and perspectives via these modeling tools."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The lifecycle of a Digital Twin (DT) or Digital Shadow (DS) in the provided context follows a structured development process, aligned with Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) principles. Key phases include:
  1. **Problem Identification**: Defining the purpose and scope of the digital representation, such as improving robot motion accuracy or detecting friction loss.
  2. **Asset and Model Analysis**: Evaluating the physical system's components, interfaces, and interconnections using architecture description languages (ADLs) like ACME, AADL, or SysML Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs) to establish a logical and functional architecture.
  3. **Use or Creation of Calculations/Models**: Developing or integrating models (e.g., multi-physics models for joint friction identification) using tools like MathWorks Simulink or general-purpose languages (C++, Java) for behavioral modeling. This phase may involve state machines (SysML v1.7) or MontiArc Automaton for dynamic behavior representation.
  4. **Data Identification**: Collecting and abstracting contextual data traces for the DS, ensuring alignment with the physical system's state. This includes aggregation and abstraction of data for specific use cases (e.g., continuous friction loss detection).
  5. **Concretization and Development**: Refining models through iterative development, ensuring they conform to the physical system's requirements and operational constraints. This may involve validation against real-world data or simulation tools.
  The lifecycle also implicitly includes **transition management** between phases, leveraging MBSE methodologies (e.g., RFLP, OOSEM, SPES, SMArDT) to handle complexity across abstraction layers (requirements → functional → logical → technical). Governance is implied through compliance with modeling standards (e.g., SysML, UML) and integration of heterogeneous stakeholder perspectives, though explicit governance frameworks are not detailed in the document."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The provided document does not explicitly address **fidelity requirements, validation/verification (V&V) methods, uncertainty quantification, error detection/correction mechanisms, or quality assurance processes** for models or data. While MBSE methodologies (e.g., SysML, ADLs) are used to describe **structure and behavior**, their application to **fidelity assurance** or **V&V** is not elaborated. The **Digital Shadow Reference Model** focuses on **one-way data flow** and **contextual data aggregation**, lacking explicit discussion of **data/model validation, uncertainty sources, or error correction**. No specifications for **tolerance thresholds, calibration protocols, or model accuracy metrics** are provided."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **technical network infrastructure** for the Digital Shadow system is partially described through the mention of **MQTT (Message Queuing Telemetry Transport)** as the protocol for streaming **time-series sensor data**. However, the document does not provide details on **network topology/architecture, bandwidth/latency requirements, reliability/redundancy mechanisms, or security protocols**. The system’s communication is limited to a **one-way data flow** from physical to digital, with no explicit bidirectional control or feedback loops. Thus, while MQTT is confirmed as a **communication protocol**, broader technical connection specifics (e.g., edge/cloud integration, protocol stacks, or fault tolerance) are **Not in Document**."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The document specifies **MQTT** as the **data exchange protocol** for streaming sensor time-series data ('We have access to time series of all sensors, which are streamed via MQTT') to the digital shadow. Horizontal integration is implied through the need to **process incoming data** from the physical system, as digital shadows are **passive** and require **external active components** (e.g., software systems) to interpret and act upon the data. While the document does not explicitly address **interoperability standards** (e.g., OPC UA, REST APIs) or integration with **other Digital Twins/enterprise systems**, it highlights the necessity of **heterogeneous model unification** (e.g., SysML, ADLs, simulation tools) to bridge stakeholder perspectives. The passive nature of digital shadows necessitates **dependency on external systems** for data interpretation, but no details on **enterprise integration patterns** (e.g., microservices, ESBs) are provided."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to **standardized modeling and interoperability frameworks**, including:
  - **SysML (Systems Modeling Language)**: Used for **systems engineering modeling** across functional, logical, and physical layers.
  - **Architecture Description Languages (ADLs)**: Such as **ACME, AADL, MontiArc, and SysML Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs)** for **component-based system description**.
  - **Unified Modeling Language (UML)**: Employed in the **Digital Shadow Reference Model** (e.g., as a **UML class diagram**) to define conceptual structures.
  - **Asset Administration Shells (AAS)**: Implemented for **standardized asset representation** in industrial contexts (though the specific **Industrial Digital Twins Association (IDTA) RAMI 4.0 framework** is not mentioned in the provided text).
  The system emphasizes **interoperability** through **multi-notation modeling** (e.g., SysML + ADLs + simulation tools) and **heterogeneous tool integration** (e.g., Simulink, C++, Java). **Certification requirements** or **compliance frameworks** are not explicitly detailed in the provided context."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The provided documents reference safety considerations in the context of **Digital Shadows** and **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** implementations within the **Automation Pyramid** (Document [42]). Specifically, the **MABI robot** (Document [44]) features a **safety cell** that restricts the maximum working range to **2.25 meters** (as per the **MAX-100-2-25-P** model specification), ensuring physical safety during operation. However, no explicit details on **cybersecurity protocols**, **access control mechanisms**, **authentication systems**, or **compliance with safety standards** (e.g., IEC 61508, IEC 62061) are provided in the documents. The **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** framework (Documents [46], [47], [50]) is mentioned as a structural component for digital twin integration but lacks specific technical descriptions of **safety-related submodels** or **risk assessment methodologies**."
]
